title: Antiviral Drugs
summary: الادوية المضادة للفيروسات تشمل: ادوية الانفلونزا مثل Oseltamivir وAmantadine، وادوية الهيرباس مثل Acyclovir وGanciclovir، وادوية الالتهاب الكبدي مثل Interferon وLamivudine، وادوية الايدز (HAART) التي تجمع NRTIs مع NNRTIs او Protease Inhibitors. Acyclovir prodrug يتفعّل بانزيمات الفيروس انتقائيا ويثبط DNA polymerase الفيروسية. HAART تجمع ادوية بآليات مختلفة لتقليل المقاومة وتحقيق قمع فيروسي عميق مع استعادة المناعة.

key_drugs.name: Oseltamivir
key_drugs.note: Oral prodrug; inhibits neuraminidase; effective influenza A&B; GIT side effects

key_drugs.name: Amantadine
key_drugs.note: Influenza A only; crosses BBB causing CNS effects; teratogenic

key_drugs.name: Rimantadine
key_drugs.note: Influenza A only; does NOT cross BBB; fewer CNS effects; longer t1/2

key_drugs.name: Ribavirin
key_drugs.note: Broad spectrum DNA & RNA viruses; teratogenic; hemolytic anemia

key_drugs.name: Acyclovir
key_drugs.note: Prodrug activated by viral thymidine kinase; DOC for HSV encephalitis; crystalluria risk

key_drugs.name: Valacyclovir
key_drugs.note: Better oral bioavailability; converts to acyclovir in body

key_drugs.name: Interferon
key_drugs.note: Hepatitis C + genital warts; induces antiviral enzymes; multiple SE

key_drugs.name: Lamivudine
key_drugs.note: Inhibits HBV DNA polymerase and HIV reverse transcriptase

key_drugs.name: Zidovudine (AZT)
key_drugs.note: First antiretroviral; myelosuppression; used in pregnancy to prevent perinatal HIV

key_drugs.name: Nevirapine
key_drugs.note: Prevent mother-to-child HIV transmission; Stevens-Johnson syndrome risk

key_drugs.name: Ritonavir
key_drugs.note: Used as pharmacokinetic booster via CYP3A inhibition; not as standalone PI

key_drugs.name: Enfuvirtide
key_drugs.note: Blocks HIV entry; SC injection; injection site reactions

key_drugs.name: Raltegravir
key_drugs.note: Inhibits HIV DNA integration into host genome

mnemonics.title: Acyclovir: المفتاح الخاص بالفيروس
mnemonics.body: Acyclovir مثل مفتاح لا يعمل الا في قفل الفيروس. اولا يفعّله انزيم thymidine kinase الموجود فقط في خلايا مصابة بالهيرباس. بعدها يصبح سلاحا يثبط DNA polymerase الفيروسية ويوقف تمدد سلسلة DNA. خلايانا السليمة لا تفعّله فلا يضرها.

mnemonics.title: HAART = 4 خطوط دفاع ضد HIV
mnemonics.body:
N = NRTIs (Nucleoside RT inhibitors: Zidovudine, Lamivudine)
N = NNRTIs (Non-nucleoside RT inhibitors: Nevirapine, Efavirenz)
P = Protease Inhibitors (navirs: Ritonavir, Indinavir)
E = Entry/Fusion inhibitors (Enfuvirtide)
I = Integrase inhibitors (Raltegravir)

mnemonics.title: Oseltamivir يحبس الفيروس
mnemonics.body: Neuraminidase مقص الفيروس الذي يقطع نفسه من الخلية ليصيب خلايا جديدة. Oseltamivir ('أوسيل') يمسك المقص ويوقفه، فيظل الفيروس محبوسا في الخلية ولا ينتشر. يجب اخذه خلال 24-48 ساعة من بدء الاعراض.

mnemonics.title: Amantadine يسكر باب التغليف
mnemonics.body: تخيل فيروس الانفلونزا A كصندوق مغلق (viral coat). Amantadine يضع قفلا (blocks uncoating) على الباب فلا يستطيع الفيروس الخروج من غلافه والدخول للخلية. لانه يخترق BBB يسبب اثارا في المخ (insomnia, ataxia, convulsions).

mnemonics.title: GRIM - اثار Ganciclovir
mnemonics.body:
G = Ganciclovir DOC for CMV
R = Retinal detachment (intraocular implants)
I = Immunosuppression (myelosuppression - neutropenia)
M = Mutagenic/teratogenic (carcinogenic potential)

flashcards.front: How do Oseltamivir and Zanamivir work?
flashcards.back: They inhibit the Neuraminidase enzyme in influenza A and B, preventing the release of newly formed virions from the infected cell and their spread.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between Amantadine and Rimantadine regarding CNS effects?
flashcards.back: Amantadine crosses the BBB, causing central effects (insomnia, ataxia, convulsions). Rimantadine does not cross the BBB, so its central effects are fewer.

flashcards.front: Why is Ribavirin contraindicated in pregnancy?
flashcards.back: Because it is teratogenic and mutagenic. It also causes dose-dependent hemolytic anemia because it accumulates in red blood cells.

flashcards.front: What is the DOC for HSV encephalitis?
flashcards.back: IV Acyclovir is the drug of choice for herpes encephalitis.

flashcards.front: How does Acyclovir act selectively against the virus?
flashcards.back: It is activated only by viral thymidine kinase found in herpes-infected cells, then it inhibits viral DNA polymerase and halts DNA chain elongation.

flashcards.front: What is crystalluria and how is it prevented with Acyclovir?
flashcards.back: Precipitation of Acyclovir crystals in the kidney causing renal dysfunction. It is prevented by ensuring adequate hydration and slow IV infusion.

flashcards.front: What is the DOC for CMV in immunocompromised patients?
flashcards.back: Ganciclovir (IV) for CMV pneumonia and retinitis in the immunocompromised. It is also used prophylactically in organ transplantation.

flashcards.front: What is the most important side effect of Ganciclovir?
flashcards.back: Myelosuppression (especially neutropenia). It increases when combined with Zidovudine or Azathioprine.

flashcards.front: How does Interferon work as an antiviral drug?
flashcards.back: It induces the synthesis of enzymes in the host cell that inhibit translation of viral mRNA into proteins and halt viral replication.

flashcards.front: What is HAART and what is its composition?
flashcards.back: Highly Active Antiretroviral Therapy: it usually combines NRTI + NRTI with an NNRTI or a Protease Inhibitor. It achieves deep viral suppression and immune restoration.

flashcards.front: How do NRTIs work?
flashcards.back: They are phosphorylated to a triphosphate that competitively inhibits HIV reverse transcriptase and terminates elongation of the viral DNA chain.

flashcards.front: What are the common side effects of NRTIs?
flashcards.back: Most of them (except Lamivudine and Abacavir) may cause lactic acidosis and fatal hepatomegaly.

flashcards.front: What is the use of Zidovudine in pregnancy?
flashcards.back: It is given to the pregnant mother during labor and to the newborn for 3 days to prevent mother-to-child transmission of HIV (perinatal transmission).

flashcards.front: What is the main risk of Nevirapine?
flashcards.back: Stevens-Johnson syndrome and Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis. Gradual dose escalation over 14 days is advised. Also fatal hepatotoxicity.

flashcards.front: Why is Ritonavir used in a small dose with another PI?
flashcards.back: Ritonavir is a potent inhibitor of CYP3A, which raises the level of the other PI, prolongs its duration of effect, and reduces resistance - this is called a pharmacokinetic booster.

flashcards.front: How does Enfuvirtide work?
flashcards.back: A fusion inhibitor that prevents HIV from fusing with and entering the host cell membrane. It is given SC.

flashcards.front: What is the characteristic side effect of Protease Inhibitors?
flashcards.back: GI disturbances, elevated triglycerides and LDL, insulin resistance, and fat redistribution (Cushingoid appearance). Serious drug interactions via CYP.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between NRTIs and NNRTIs in mechanism of action?
flashcards.back: NRTIs require phosphorylation for activation and inhibit RT competitively. NNRTIs do not require phosphorylation and bind directly to a site near RT. There is no cross-resistance between them.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Oseltamivir/Zanamivir?
flashcards.back: Neuraminidase inhibitors prevent release of virions; effective against influenza A and B.

flashcards.front: When should Oseltamivir be started?
flashcards.back: Within 24-48 hours of symptom onset; Oseltamivir is an oral prodrug.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Amantadine?
flashcards.back: It inhibits viral uncoating; influenza A only; central CNS effects.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Acyclovir?
flashcards.back: Activated by viral thymidine kinase → inhibits DNA polymerase; selective for infected cells.

flashcards.front: What is the toxicity of Acyclovir?
flashcards.back: Nephrotoxicity (maintain hydration).

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Ribavirin?
flashcards.back: A guanosine analogue that inhibits replication of RNA and DNA viruses; teratogenic and causes hemolytic anemia.

flashcards.front: What is the modern treatment of hepatitis C?
flashcards.back: DAAs (NS3/4A inhibitors '-previr', NS5A and NS5B inhibitors), often ± Ribavirin; interferon has become rare.

flashcards.front: What are the classes of anti-HIV drugs?
flashcards.back: NRTI (Zidovudine), NNRTI, protease inhibitors, integrase inhibitors.

mcqs.q: What is the mechanism of action of Oseltamivir in influenza?
options: It prevents fusion of the virus with the cell
It inhibits Neuraminidase, preventing the release of newly formed virions
It prevents uncoating of the viral envelope
mcqs.explanation: Oseltamivir and Zanamivir inhibit Neuraminidase, which prevents the virus from leaving the infected cell and infecting new cells.

mcqs.q: An influenza A patient has renal problems. Which is better: Amantadine or Rimantadine?
options: Amantadine because it is excreted hepatically
Rimantadine because it is metabolized hepatically and does not depend on the kidney
Amantadine because it is more potent
mcqs.explanation: Rimantadine is extensively metabolized hepatically and has a longer t1/2, whereas Amantadine is excreted in the urine, so it must be used cautiously in renal failure.

mcqs.q: What is the drug of choice for HSV encephalitis?
mcqs.explanation: IV Acyclovir is the DOC for herpes encephalitis (HSV encephalitis). It distributes well and crosses the BBB.

mcqs.q: What is the DOC for CMV retinitis in an AIDS patient?
mcqs.explanation: Ganciclovir is the DOC for CMV in immunocompromised patients (CMV pneumonia and retinitis).

mcqs.q: A pregnant mother is infected with HIV. Which drug is used to prevent transmission of the virus to her fetus?
options: Zidovudine for the mother then for the newborn
mcqs.explanation: Zidovudine is given to the mother during labor and to the newborn for 3 days to prevent perinatal HIV transmission. Nevirapine is an alternative.

mcqs.q: What is the most common side effect of Ganciclovir?
mcqs.explanation: Myelosuppression, especially neutropenia, is the most important side effect of Ganciclovir and increases with Zidovudine.

mcqs.q: What distinguishes NNRTIs from NRTIs in their action?
options: NNRTIs bind directly to RT without the need for phosphorylation
There is no difference between them
mcqs.explanation: NNRTIs bind directly to a site near HIV-1 reverse transcriptase without the need for phosphorylation, unlike NRTIs.

mcqs.q: A patient on HAART develops a Cushingoid appearance and elevated TG. Which class is most likely responsible?
mcqs.explanation: Protease Inhibitors cause fat redistribution (Cushingoid), elevated TG and LDL, and insulin resistance.

mcqs.q: Why is Ritonavir added in a small dose to another PI in HAART?
options: To directly enhance its antiviral activity
As a pharmacokinetic booster by inhibiting CYP3A, raising the level of the other PI
Because it is the most potent in the class
mcqs.explanation: Ritonavir is a potent inhibitor of CYP3A4 that raises the level of the other PI (pharmacokinetic booster), allowing lower doses and longer intervals and reducing resistance.

mcqs.q: Ribavirin is contraindicated in pregnancy because of?
mcqs.explanation: Ribavirin is contraindicated in pregnancy because of its documented teratogenicity and mutagenicity.
It prevents the release of virions.

mcqs.q: Acyclovir is selective because it is activated by:
mcqs.explanation: The viral enzyme phosphorylates it first.

high_yield.point: Oseltamivir prodrug فموي فعّال؛ يجب البدء خلال 48 ساعة من الأعراض.
Acyclovir يُفعَّل بإنزيم thymidine kinase الفيروسي → انتقائي للخلايا المصابة؛ سُمّية كلوية.
Amantadine يثبّط نزع الغلاف (uncoating)؛ آثار عصبية مركزية + ماسخ.
التهاب الكبد C: Interferon-β + Ribavirin (أو DAAs الحديثة).
Ribavirin ماسخ ومُسبب لفقر دم انحلالي → ممنوع بالحمل.
